rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 8, 2023 line 1. NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient Notice. The Court notes that the Amended Petition does not provide any statutory basis for a relief demanded via a petition procedure in a civil action as opposed to a complaint, which would require an issuance of summons. If petitioner re-notices the petition for hearing, the statutory authority for the petition proceeding shall be included. Furthermore, petitioner must properly serve the respondent/defendant. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER LUCAS VS. EDMUND N. LUCAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 8, 2023 line 3. PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER LUCAS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication Of Issues is DENIED. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. No issues to be adjudicated are stated in the Notice of Motion or Separate Statement. CRC 3.1350(b). Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Plaintiff included in the separate statement as material facts, facts number 8, 9, and 10. These facts are disputed, and the dispute is supported by admissible evidence. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
